audit_items.proof_trace: The file declares `_lock` as a value-typed member (`OSSpinLock` on macOS, `pthread_spinlock_t` on Linux) — no raw pointer variables exist anywhere in spin_lock.h. All spin-lock API calls pass `&_lock` (address-of a member), which is guaranteed non-null as long as the object itself is valid. There are no explicit pointer dereferences (`*ptr`) in the file, so the null-dereference invariant holds unconditionally.
